data.description:
# Docs: gate behavior, config surface, cert semantics, CI-check pattern

## Why
A polyglot, config-driven gate used by a diverse client base is unusable without clear docs on how it works, what is configurable, how fail-open behaves, what the certificate means, and how CI checks it.

## What
End-user + integrator documentation for the plan-review gate.

## Scope
- How the gate works (two layers, exec tiers, the claim->review->revise->re-sign loop).
- The config surface (chunking, budget caps, per-criterion blocking, risk posture, overlay triggers).
- Fail-open behavior and coverage reporting.
- Certificate semantics (what the signature asserts and does NOT assert) + the CI-check pattern.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A docs page covering the gate lifecycle, exec tiers, and the revise loop.
- [ ] A documented config reference for every knob with its default and override path.
- [ ] Documentation of fail-open behavior, cert semantics, and the CI rigor-check pattern.
data.title: Docs: gate behavior, config surface, cert semantics, CI-check pattern
